name: CHA2DS2-VASc
full_name: Congestive Heart Failure, Hypertension, Age ≥75 (doubled), Diabetes, Stroke (doubled), Vascular Disease, Age 65-74, Sex Category
description: Estimates stroke risk in patients with atrial fibrillation.
purpose: Guide anticoagulation therapy decisions in non-valvular atrial fibrillation.
when_to_use: Deciding whether to initiate anticoagulation therapy
when_not_to_use: Patients with valvular atrial fibrillation (e.g., mitral stenosis)

items.label: chf
items.description: Congestive heart failure (or left ventricular systolic dysfunction)

items.label: hypertension
items.description: Hypertension (resting BP >140/90 mmHg on at least 2 occasions or current antihypertensive treatment)

items.label: diabetes
items.description: Diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose >125 mg/dL or treatment with oral hypoglycemic agent and/or insulin)

items.label: vascular_disease
items.description: Vascular disease (prior MI, peripheral artery disease, or aortic plaque)

formula: chf + hypertension + age_75 + diabetes + stroke_tia + vascular_disease + age_65_74 + female

interpretation.description: Low risk — 0.2% annual stroke risk. Anticoagulation generally not recommended.
Low-moderate risk — 0.6% annual stroke risk. Consider anticoagulation (especially if not due to female sex alone).
Moderate risk — 2.2-3.2% annual stroke risk. Oral anticoagulation recommended.
Moderate-high risk — 4.8-6.7% annual stroke risk. Oral anticoagulation recommended.
High risk — 7.2-12.2% annual stroke risk. Oral anticoagulation strongly recommended.

references.citation: Lip GY, Nieuwlaat R, Pisters R, Lane DA, Crijns HJ. Refining clinical risk stratification for predicting stroke and thromboembolism in atrial fibrillation using a novel risk factor-based approach: the euro heart survey on atrial fibrillation. Chest. 2010;137(2):263-272.

constraints.description: Patient can only be in one age group

notes: A score of 1 in females due solely to sex category may not warrant anticoagulation. Age ≥75 and prior stroke/TIA carry the highest individual weights (2 points each).
